text:
The Meteor City's Elder (, Ryūseigai no Chōrō) was a member of Meteor City's Assembly. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 352 

Appearance

The Meteor City's Elder dressed in dirty ragged clothing that nearly covered up his entire body. He also wore a mouthpiece that covered his nose and mouth. 

Personality

Little is known about the Elder but he considered the dichotomy between humans and puppets was little, and as a Meteor City resident, he was likely influenced by the conditions of there, sharing remarkable bonds that have been described as "thinner than water, and yet thicker than blood". Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 102 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Amid his battle with Hisoka, Chrollo reveals that his Nen ability The Sun and Moon originally belonged to the Meteor City's Elder. The elder used the ability to send a "message" by using a fellow resident of the city at any aggressors who have done harm in any way to any resident of the city. 

Abilities & Powers

Being part of the Assembly of Elders it is likely that he has some political influence on Meteor City.

Nen

It is not known what the proficiency of the Meteor City's Elder in Nen is, but he is skilled enough to have created a Nen ability. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

The method of using human bombs as means of revenge occurred in The Bum Incident incident related to Meteor City. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 102 It is possible that the Elder was the one who turned them into one, given that a scene of a smoking crater caused by some sort of an explosion is seen somewhere in an unnamed city. 

Intertextuality and References

The Sun and Moon is similar in appearance and effects to the style of alchemy used by Solf J. Kimblee in the Fullmetal Alchemist series.
